title: AGENTS.md
text: Guidance coding agents working repository.

title: AGENTS.md
text: R package named weeknumber. package implements vctrs-based weeknumber class helpers, formatting, arithmetic, casting, ggplot2 scale support. Package metadata lives DESCRIPTION; exported objects registered NAMESPACE.

title: AGENTS.md
text: R/: package source files. man/: generated Rd documentation. tests/testthat/: unit tests. tests/testthat.R: testthat entrypoint. README.Rmd: source README. README.md: generated README.Rmd; edit hand. .github/workflows/: CI definitions R-CMD-check pkgdown.

title: AGENTS.md
text: Make focused changes preserve existing package structure naming. Prefer editing relevant file R/ adding updating tests tests/testthat/ behavior changes. Keep function style consistent existing codebase: small functions, explicit namespace qualification already used, straightforward base R/vectorized logic. hand-edit generated outputs unless user explicitly asks .

title: A Class for Week Numbers

title: A Class for Week Numbers
text: Install GitHub:

title: A Class for Week Numbers
text: Coercion week number class: Make week number object year week: Get year week number object: Print tibble: Plot week number scale:
code: library(weeknumber) as_weeknumber(c(-1:1, 51:52, NA)) #> <weeknumber[6]> #> [1] 1999-W52 2000-W01 2000-W02 2000-W52 2001-W01 <NA> as_weeknumber("2000-W01") #> <weeknumber[1]> #> [1] 2000-W01 as_weeknumber(as.Date("2000-12-28")) #> <weeknumber[1]> #> [1] 2000-W52 make_weeknumber(2000:2001, 4:5) #> <weeknumber[2]> #> [1] 2000-W04 2001-W05 make_weeknumber(2019:2020, 53) #> <weeknumber[2]> #> [1] <NA>     2020-W53 x <- as_weeknumber(c(-1:1, 51:52, NA)) year_week(x) #> $year #> [1] 1999 2000 2000 2000 2001   NA #>  #> $week #> [1] 52  1  2 52  1 NA set.seed(0) library(tibble) d <- tibble(   x = seq(as_weeknumber("2000-W01"), as_weeknumber("2000-W09")),   y = cumsum(rnorm(length(x))) ) print(d) #> # A tibble: 9 × 2 #>          x     y #>     <week> <dbl> #> 1 2000-W01 1.26  #> 2 2000-W02 0.937 #> 3 2000-W03 2.27  #> 4 2000-W04 3.54  #> 5 2000-W05 3.95  #> 6 2000-W06 2.41  #> 7 2000-W07 1.49  #> 8 2000-W08 1.19  #> 9 2000-W09 1.18 library(ggplot2) p <- ggplot(d, aes(x, y)) +   geom_line() print(p)

title: Make weeknumber from ISO year and week — make_weeknumber
text: Input arguments recycled common size, using vctrs::vec_recycle_common(). Weeks outside valid range corresponding year result NA.

what: Low-level constructor for weeknumber vectors — new_weeknumber
title: Low-level constructor for weeknumber vectors — new_weeknumber
text: new_weeknumber() low-level constructor creates weeknumber vectors underlying double representation.

title: Low-level constructor for weeknumber vectors — new_weeknumber
Low-level constructor for weeknumber vectors — new_weeknumber
text: x double vector ISO week offsets 2000-W01.

title: Low-level constructor for weeknumber vectors — new_weeknumber
text: weeknumber vector.

title: Low-level constructor for weeknumber vectors — new_weeknumber
text: underlying double values count ISO weeks relative 2000-W01, 0 represents 2000-W01, 1 represents 2000-W02, . Non-finite values converted NA_real_. constructor follows vctrs convention validating underlying storage type. Use weeknumber() user-facing helper is_weeknumber() test class.

title: Low-level constructor for weeknumber vectors — new_weeknumber

what: Position scales for weeknumber vectors — scale_weeknumber
title: Position scales for weeknumber vectors — scale_weeknumber
text: scale_x_weeknumber() scale_y_weeknumber() create continuous ggplot2 position scales weeknumber data x y axes.

title: Position scales for weeknumber vectors — scale_weeknumber
code: scale_x_weeknumber(   name = ggplot2::waiver(),   breaks = ggplot2::waiver(),   minor_breaks = ggplot2::waiver(),   labels = ggplot2::waiver(),   limits = NULL,   expand = ggplot2::waiver(),   oob = scales::censor,   na.value = NA_real_,   position = "bottom" )  scale_y_weeknumber(   name = ggplot2::waiver(),   breaks = ggplot2::waiver(),   minor_breaks = ggplot2::waiver(),   labels = ggplot2::waiver(),   limits = NULL,   expand = ggplot2::waiver(),   oob = scales::censor,   na.value = NA_real_,   position = "left" )

title: Position scales for weeknumber vectors — scale_weeknumber
text: name, breaks, minor_breaks, labels, limits, expand, oob, na.value, position Passed ggplot2::scale_x_continuous() ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(). See functions details.

title: Position scales for weeknumber vectors — scale_weeknumber
text: ggplot2 position scale weeknumber data.

title: Position scales for weeknumber vectors — scale_weeknumber
text: helpers use package's weeknumber transformation ggplot2 can plot weeknumber vectors directly preserving weeknumber values break calculations labels. default, breaks chosen sensible weekly, monthly-ish, quarterly-ish, yearly intervals across displayed range.

title: Position scales for weeknumber vectors — scale_weeknumber
code: df <- data.frame(   week = make_weeknumber(2024, 1:6),   value = c(3, 4, 2, 5, 6, 4) )  ggplot2::ggplot(df, ggplot2::aes(week, value)) +   ggplot2::geom_line() +   scale_x_weeknumber()   ggplot2::ggplot(df, ggplot2::aes(value, week)) +   ggplot2::geom_point() +   scale_y_weeknumber()
